_escalationProvider_note: OPTIONAL second LOCAL model used ONLY to regenerate escalation options (rare, worth a bigger model). Omit to use `provider` for everything. Must be a loopback endpoint (localhost/127.0.0.1) — a remote URL is refused to stay subscription-safe. Tip: make `provider` a small/fast model for every-turn triage and set this to a bigger one. LIVE FINDING (2026-06-30): a 30B heavy model added ~24s/escalation and did NOT beat qwen3.5:9b's already-strong escalation options on test scenarios — leave this OFF unless your heavy model is both fast AND clearly sharper. The fast model's escalations are good enough.
_brain_note: Brain-decision policy. confidenceThreshold (0..1, default 0 = off): if a 'continue' decision's self-reported confidence is below it, the operator auto-escalates to you instead of guessing the next step. LIVE FINDING (2026-06-30): qwen3.5:9b reports HIGH confidence (0.9–0.95) most of the time and ESCALATES on its own when genuinely unsure rather than emitting a low-confidence 'continue' — so this gate rarely fires. Keep it LOW (~0.5) if you use it at all; it's a backstop, not a main control. rollingSummary (default off) — RECOMMENDED ON for long runs: feed the brain a maintained running summary + a short fresh tail instead of the raw last-8 messages. Live-validated accurate (captured all built features + a bug fix in 464 chars). everyTurns = how often to re-summarize (one local-LLM call); tailMessages = raw messages still passed alongside; maxChars caps the summary. NOTE: git ground-truth (REPO STATE) is always-on (no knob) and was the strongest win in validation — it caught an agent falsely claiming a commit.
_templates_note: Reusable session presets — pick one in the dashboard's New Session wizard (or the Templates manager) to one-click pre-fill a new session, leaving only the working folder to choose. 'Save as template' snapshots an existing session's settings here. Managed live from the UI; this is just the on-disk shape. Omit entirely if you don't use them.

templates.name: Bug-fix sprint
templates.description: Triage and fix open bugs until the suite is green.
templates.goal: Find and fix the failing tests / open bugs in this project.
templates.doneCriteria: The full test suite passes and there are no known regressions.

_registry_note: OPTIONAL remote template registry (the marketplace network layer). OFF unless you set a url. 'url' (GET) returns a JSON array of recipes (or {"recipes":[…]}) to browse + install in the Templates manager; 'publishUrl' (POST) is where 'Publish' sends a local template; 'token' is an optional bearer token sent on both. This carries TEMPLATE DATA ONLY and is entirely separate from the local-only decision brain — it never affects subscription-safety. Publishing sends content to an external service, so it's always a deliberate, confirmed click. Omit this whole block to keep everything local.
_automations_note: OPTIONAL automation rules — the reactive layer over webhooks + dependencies. Each rule: when a session hits a lifecycle event ('on'; omit = any of done/error/stopped/needs-input/rate-limited), optionally only for a matching firing session ('match'), run 'actions' in order. An action is {kind:'start'|'stop', target} (target is a session id or '$self' = the firing session), {kind:'setMode', target, mode:'manual'|'autopilot'} (flip a session's mode), {kind:'sendMessage', target, message} (inject a message when that session is awaiting input), {kind:'notify', message?} (fires your configured webhooks, optionally with a custom message), or {kind:'webhook', webhook} (fire one specific webhook by name). start/stop reuse the normal supervisor paths, so all gating/queueing applies; a rule can't start the firing session itself (loop guard). Local-only — no model calls. Manage these in the dashboard's Automations panel. Omit this block for none.

automations.name: Deploy after the API build finishes

actions.message: A session errored — paused for review.

_quietHours_note: OPTIONAL notification quiet hours — silence the sound alarm and outbound webhooks during a daily LOCAL-time window. 'start'/'end' are 'HH:MM' (24h); an end earlier than the start spans midnight (e.g. 22:00 -> 07:00). 'days' (0=Sun..6=Sat, keyed by the window's start day) restricts which nights it applies to — omit/empty = every day. 'allowUrgent' lets 'error' notifications page you even while quiet. Operational automations are never affected (only the human-facing notification is gated). Edit this in the dashboard's Settings panel. Omit the block (or set enabled:false) for none.
_automationChainCap_note: OPTIONAL loop guard for cascading automation rules. When rule X starts B, B's completion fires rule Y that starts C, and so on, an accidental cycle would fire forever. Each session carries the causal 'generation' of its run (0 for a user/schedule/dependency start; parent+1 when an automation started/affected it); once a hop would exceed this many generations the whole batch is dropped and logged in the automation history. Default 8. Set <= 0 to disable the cap (not recommended).
_workflowDepthCap_note: OPTIONAL manual-review guard for deep dependency workflows. A workflow's depth is the longest chain of sequential 'runs-after' steps; once a chain passes this many steps the orchestrator stops auto-promoting it and parks the next step as 'needs review' so you start it by hand (your click is the review). The builder also warns when a drawn edge would push a chain past this. Default 10. Set <= 0 to disable (auto-run chains of any depth). Edit in the dashboard's Settings panel.

sessions.goal: Build a simple one-page marketing website for my friend's coffee shop: hero, menu, hours, location map embed, contact form. Plain HTML/CSS, no framework.
sessions.doneCriteria: index.html exists and renders all five sections with placeholder content, looks reasonable, and has no obvious broken markup.

sessions.goal: Deploy the finished coffee-shop site to GitHub Pages and verify the live URL loads.
sessions.doneCriteria: The site is published and the public URL returns the homepage.

_notes.provider.model: must match what the local server reports under /v1/models
_notes.provider.model.note: the brain runs once per turn — pick a FAST model. qwen3.5:9b benchmarked ~2-3s/decision @ 88% accuracy with good escalation; the 35B was 27-52s (too slow), nemotron-nano 192s (unusable).
_notes.permissionMode: acceptEdits auto-accepts file edits; 'bypassPermissions' = fully unattended (riskier); 'default' = prompts more (needed for gatePolicy to see bash prompts)
_notes.gatePolicy: 'guard' (default) escalates dangerous gates (rm -rf, force-push, secrets…) for human approve/deny and default-denies if unattended; 'auto' approves everything. Only effective when claude actually prompts — use permissionMode 'default'.
_notes.limits: the real budget is rate-limit/turn burn, not money — keep maxTurns sane while testing. stuckTurns: escalate if no files change for N turns in a row (0 = off).
_notes.budget: daily caps across ALL sessions (resets at local midnight). Sessions refuse to start / stop when exceeded. Omit to disable. The dashboard shows today's usage; claude's own usage-limit notice also auto-pauses the session.
_notes.maxConcurrent: max sessions running at once; extra 'Start all' sessions queue and auto-start as slots free (protects the rate limit). Omit for no cap.
_notes.contextGuard: memory-preserving compaction for long runs. When estimated context use (transcript bytes/4 ÷ window) crosses compactAtPercent, the agent writes a handoff to handoffFile, runs /compact, then resumes from the handoff — so it doesn't overflow or lose the thread. Set enabled:true and window to your model's context (e.g. 200000, or 1000000). minTurnsBetween throttles repeat compactions.
_notes.learning: self-improvement: Qwen learns YOUR operating style from past Claude Code sessions + your live corrections, behind a propose→approve→revert gate (use the dashboard 'Learn' button). OFF by default and a strict no-op until you approve a profile. enabled:true turns on guidance injection for APPROVED profiles only; the other knobs bound mining/few-shot/guidance size + the advisory eval's held-out set.
_notes.dispatch: remote access for when you expose the dashboard port to reach it from your phone. Set token (or env AGI_DISPATCH_TOKEN) to a strong secret — with NO token, remote requests are refused (local always works). Remote clients send it as Authorization: Bearer, X-AGI-Token, or ?token=. trustLocal:true (default) skips the token for loopback; set it false if you front the dashboard with a LOCAL tunnel (cloudflared/ngrok) so the token is still required. The token travels in the request — prefer a TLS tunnel (Tailscale / Cloudflare Tunnel) over bare HTTP. rateLimit caps remote requests/IP and (stricter) auth failures/IP to thwart brute force.
_notes.usageGuard: hooks into Claude's REAL subscription limits instead of an artificial daily cap. The orchestrator reads Claude's own /usage (a local command, no model usage) and pauses a session when a governing limit is spent, auto-resuming at the reported reset. pauseAtPercent (default 100) pauses earlier if set lower. onOpusExhausted: when the weekly all-models (Opus) cap is spent but the Sonnet pool has room, 'continue' keeps working on Sonnet (default) and 'pause' stops until the weekly reset. refreshEveryTurns re-reads /usage every N turns. When enabled, the legacy budget.maxTurnsPerDay/maxMinutesPerDay no longer gate runs.
_notes.gatePolicy.note: see permissionMode — gatePolicy only sees gates when claude actually prompts
_notes.autonomy: operator persona: 'cautious' escalates more decisions to you, 'autonomous' fewer, 'balanced' (default) in between
_notes.dependsOn: workflows: ids of sessions that must reach 'done' before this one auto-starts. 'Start all' launches the roots and parks dependents as 'blocked'; each finishes → its dependents auto-start (a DAG, cycles are rejected). Omit for no dependencies. Edit it live from the session form's 'Runs after' picker. 'Stop all' halts the whole fleet (running, queued, and blocked).
_notes.templates: reusable session presets (name + goal + doneCriteria + permissionMode + autonomy + startMode, minus cwd). Use one to pre-fill the New Session wizard, or 'Save as template' to capture an existing session. Managed from the dashboard Templates manager.
_notes.logging: structured logging for unattended runs. level: debug|info|warn|error (default info). file: append rotating JSON-lines here (omit for console-only); it rotates to file.1…file.N when it would exceed maxBytes (default 5 MiB), keeping maxFiles (default 5). Console output is unchanged; the file is the durable record for post-mortems.
_notes.reliability: self-healing knobs. retries: transient brain-call retry attempts (0–10, default 3) with doubling backoff from retryBackoffMs (default 400). brainPollSeconds: how often to re-check an unreachable local model while auto-paused (5–300, default 15). Tunable live from Settings.
